sanskrit:
मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय।
निवसिष्यसि मय्येवात ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः॥
transliteration:
mayy eva mana ādhatsva mayi buddhiṃ niveśaya |
nivasiṣyasi mayyevāta ūrdhvaṃ na saṃśayaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Just fix the mind on Me alone; settle the intellect in Me alone. You will thereafter dwell in Me alone—no doubt about it.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a single-pointed attentional protocol: place all cognitive and emotional bandwidth on the sacred center (Me). The promise is experiential union—living inside the resourceful state rather than observing it from the outside.
psychological_state: Flow-state invitation: narrow attentional aperture to one internalized secure base, collapsing subject-object split.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver told by her therapist to stop multitasking and, for three minutes, breathe while visualizing the face of her infant—nothing else.
anchor_text: Rest your whole mind and heart in Me alone; live inside My peace.
